# Sirr (سر) — Ephemeral Secret Vault

Sirr is a self-hosted ephemeral secret vault. Single Rust binary, zero runtime
dependencies, end-to-end encrypted with ChaCha20Poly1305.

## What It Does

- Store secrets with TTL (time-to-live) and/or read limits
- Secrets auto-delete when expired or after N reads
- Simple auth model: API keys as owner credentials; reads are universal
- Visibility modes control who can create secrets (public/private/both/none)
- Admin via Unix domain socket (no master API key, no network exposure)
- Webhooks: per-key, fire-and-forget HTTP POST on lifecycle events
- Audit logging for all secret operations
- CLI, HTTP API, Node.js SDK, Python SDK, .NET SDK, MCP server

## Primary Use Cases

- Share database credentials with AI assistants that auto-expire (read-once)
- CI/CD tokens that burn after a single deploy
- Team secret management with simple key-based ownership
- Contractor API access with automatic expiry

## Visibility Model

Four modes set at startup or changed at runtime via admin socket:

- `public`  — anyone can push; reads are universal (no auth needed for reads)
- `private` — only API key holders can push; reads remain open
- `both`    — both anonymous and keyed pushes allowed; keyed secrets are owned
- `none`    — lockdown: all six HTTP endpoints return 503

Knowing a secret's hash IS the read capability. No permission letters, no roles.

## HTTP API — Six Endpoints

POST   /secret            Create a secret. Bearer token → owned; no token → anonymous dead drop.
GET    /secret/:hash      Read + consume. Returns plaintext or JSON. 410 if burned/expired.
HEAD   /secret/:hash      Metadata only. Does NOT consume a read.
PATCH  /secret/:hash      Update value (owner key required).
DELETE /secret/:hash      Burn immediately (owner key or anonymous for unowned).
GET    /secret/:hash/audit  Full event history (owner key required).
GET    /secrets           List all secrets owned by the calling key. Auth required (401 if anonymous). Includes burned tombstones until pruned.

### Create Request

POST /secret
Authorization: Bearer <api-key>   (optional)
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "value": "postgres://...",
  "ttl_seconds": 3600,
  "reads": 1,
  "prefix": "db-"
}

### Create Response (201)

{
  "hash": "db-a3f8c9d1...",
  "url": "https://sirr.sirrlock.com/secret/db-a3f8c9d1...",
  "expires_at": 1700003600,
  "reads_remaining": 1,
  "owned": true
}

### HEAD Response Headers (200 if active, 410 if gone)

X-Sirr-Created: 2024-01-15T10:00:00Z
X-Sirr-TTL-Expires: 2024-01-15T11:00:00Z    (if TTL set)
X-Sirr-Reads-Remaining: 3                   (if read-limited)
X-Sirr-Owned: true

### Audit Response (200)

{
  "hash": "db-a3f8c9d1...",
  "created_at": 1700000000,
  "events": [
    {"type": "secret.create", "at": 1700000000, "ip": ""},
    {"type": "secret.read",   "at": 1700001234, "ip": ""}
  ]
}

### List Response (200) — GET /secrets

Array of secret metadata. Never includes values or ciphertext.

[
  {
    "hash": "db1_abc123...",
    "created_at": 1712846400,
    "ttl_expires_at": 1712850000,
    "reads_remaining": 3,
    "burned": false,
    "burned_at": null,
    "owned": true
  }
]

Errors: 401 if no/invalid token, 503 if visibility=none.

## CLI — sirr (client)

sirr push "value"                         Push anonymous dead drop
sirr push "value" --reads 1 --ttl 1h     With limits
sirr push "value" --key <token>           Owned push
sirr push "value" --prefix db-           With hash prefix

sirr get <hash>                           Read value (consumes a read)
sirr get <hash> --json                    JSON output
sirr inspect <hash>                       Metadata only (HEAD)
sirr audit <hash> --key <token>           Audit trail (owner required)
sirr patch <hash> "new-value" --key <t>   Update value (owner required)
sirr burn <hash>                          Burn anonymous secret
sirr burn <hash> --key <token>            Burn owned secret
sirr list --key <token>                   List all owned secrets (metadata only)

# Global flags:
--server http://...    or SIRR_SERVER=...
--key <api-token>      or SIRR_KEY=...

## CLI — sirrd (admin, via Unix socket)

sirrd serve                               Start server (default: visibility=public)
sirrd serve --visibility private          Start in private mode
sirrd serve --port 7843 --socket /tmp/sirrd.sock

sirrd visibility set public|private|both|none
sirrd visibility get

sirrd keys create <name>                  Create API key (prints token once)
sirrd keys create <name> --webhook-url https://...
sirrd keys list
sirrd keys delete <name>

sirrd audit [--limit 100]                 Query all audit events

## Webhooks

Per-key, fire-and-forget. Set webhook_url when creating a key:

sirrd keys create my-key --webhook-url https://hooks.example.com/sirr

Events fire after: secret.created, secret.read, secret.patched, secret.burned.
Anonymous secrets never fire webhooks.

Payload:
{
  "type": "secret.created",
  "hash": "db-a3f8c9d1...",
  "at": 1700000000,
  "ip": ""
}

## Environment Variables

### Server (sirrd)

SIRR_VISIBILITY       Starting visibility: public|private|both|none (default: public)
SIRR_PORT             HTTP port (default: 7843)
SIRR_HOST             Bind address (default: 0.0.0.0)
SIRR_DATA_DIR         Storage dir (platform default, or /data in Docker)
SIRR_ADMIN_SOCKET     Unix socket path (default: /tmp/sirrd.sock)
SIRR_RETENTION_DAYS   Tombstone retention before prune (default: 30)
SIRR_LOG_LEVEL        trace/debug/info/warn/error (default: info)

### Client (sirr)

SIRR_SERVER           Server URL (default: https://sirr.sirrlock.com)
SIRR_KEY              Bearer API token for authenticated operations

## SDKs

### Node.js (@sirr/sdk)
import { SirrClient } from '@sirr/sdk'
const c = new SirrClient({ server: '...', key: '...' })
await c.push('value', { ttl: 3600, reads: 1 })
const v = await c.get('hash')

### Python (sirr)
from sirr import SirrClient
c = SirrClient(server='...', key='...')
r = c.push('value', ttl=3600, reads=1)
v = c.get(r.hash)

### .NET (Sirr.Client)
var client = new SirrClient(new SirrOptions { Server = "...", Key = "..." });
var result = await client.PushAsync("value");
var v = await client.GetAsync(result.Hash);

## MCP Server (@sirrlock/mcp)

Configure in .mcp.json:
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "sirr": {
      "command": "sirr-mcp",
      "env": { "SIRR_SERVER": "http://localhost:7843", "SIRR_KEY": "..." }
    }
  }
}

Tools: push_secret, get_secret, inspect_secret, burn_secret, audit_secret

## Security Model

- ChaCha20Poly1305 encrypts each secret value with a random per-record nonce
- Encryption key is a random 32-byte file (sirr.key), generated on first boot
- Metadata (TTL, read count) stored plaintext for efficient expiry scanning
- Constant-time bearer token comparison prevents timing attacks
- Knowing the hash is the read capability — no separate auth for reads
- Ownership enforced by key ID stored in the secret record
- Admin authentication via Unix socket filesystem permissions (no network)
- No telemetry, no phone-home

## Storage

- sirr.db   — redb embedded key-value database (single file)
- sirr.key  — 32-byte encryption key, generated on first run

## Repository Layout

crates/sirr-server/   Rust library: axum server, redb store, crypto, authz, webhooks
crates/sirr/          Rust binary: CLI client (clap + reqwest)
crates/sirrd/         Rust binary: server daemon + admin socket

## Licensing

BSL 1.1: source-available, honor-system, converts to Apache 2.0 on February 20, 2028.
No enforcement code, no license server calls.
License keys at: https://sirrlock.com/pricing
